items.title: Top OMCs post losses with government weighing relief measures
items.summary: State-run oil companies HPCL and BPCL reported significant losses for the April-June quarter. This occurred as they sold petrol, diesel, and LPG below market rates. Elevated crude prices, driven by West Asia conflict, impacted their earnings. The oil ministry is now working on a compensation proposal for these companies. The companies' financial outlook depends on global oil price trajectory and potential government support.

items.title: Godrej Capital enters gold loan business with Kanakadurga Finance acquisition
items.summary: Godrej Capital acquired Kanakadurga Finance's gold loan business to expand its consumer finance segment. This acquisition aims to build a Rs 1 lakh crore assets under management franchise by 2031. Kanakadurga Finance's business adds 54 branches and 250 employees to Godrej Capital. The gold loan segment is experiencing significant growth due to rising gold prices. Godrej Capital also launched its wealth management business earlier this year.

items.title: Trump vows to bomb Iran's bridges, power plants if it shoots at ships in Hormuz Strait
items.summary: He issued his threat after U.S. Central Command hit Iranian targets for an 11th straight night.

items.title: Recently listed Indo SMC bags ₹66.6 crore order from engineering firm; stock jumps 190% from March lows
items.summary: Indo SMC announced a ₹66.60 crore order for Variable Frequency Drive panels from an Indian engineering company. The order is to be completed by July 31, 2026. The company also reported a net profit increase to ₹32 crore for FY26 and a significant surge in stock price.

items.title: NAN MagneTech to invest Rs 1,250 crore in Andhra Pradesh for rare earth magnet plant
items.summary: NAN MagneTech will invest Rs 1,250 crore in Andhra Pradesh for a new facility. This plant will be India's first integrated rare earth magnet manufacturing unit. Commercial production is expected to begin in early 2028. The facility will produce magnets for electric vehicles and wind turbines. This project aims to reduce India's reliance on imports.

items.title: AT&T shares jump 4% after strong Q2 earnings, raising buyback target
items.summary: AT&T shares climbed after reporting robust second-quarter earnings and adding customers. The company announced plans to accelerate its share buyback program this year. Revenue and income from continuing operations saw year-on-year increases. AT&T added over one million advanced connectivity customers during the quarter. The telecom giant reiterated its financial guidance through 2028.

items.title: RBI sold $6.1 bn in May as oil weighed on the rupee and foreign inflows showed signs of revival: Bulletin
items.summary: The Reserve Bank of India sold six point one billion dollars in May. This intervention occurred amid global market volatility and geopolitical tensions. India's external sector remains steady with improving foreign investment inflows. The domestic economy navigated uncertainties well, supported by strong demand. Foreign exchange reserves are comfortable, covering over ten months of imports.

items.title: Temasek's India exposure falls to USD 42 bln in FY26; company says India important market
items.summary: Temasek's India exposure fell to USD 42 billion in FY26 from USD 50 billion. This decline was mainly due to a large exit from Schneider Electric. Executives stressed India remains an important and optimistic destination for the firm. The company is overweight on India, with allocations higher than its GDP share. Temasek continues to seek portfolio growth opportunities across various sectors.

The incident occurred after a suspected methane gas explosion trapped workers inside. NHPC will provide ex-gratia payments to the families of the deceased. The company expressed profound grief over the tragic loss of lives.

items.summary: Chirag Paswan said India must now shift from food self-reliance to value addition through processing, infrastructure, innovation and better farmer market access.

items.title: RBI sold $6 billion in May to defend oil-hit Indian rupee, data shows
items.summary: The Reserve Bank of India sold $6.1 billion in foreign exchange during May. This intervention occurred as oil prices surged, pushing the rupee to a record low. The central bank also implemented policy measures to encourage dollar inflows into India.

items.summary: U.S. markets opened mixed as Nasdaq and S&P 500 slipped on chip stock weakness, while Dow edged higher. Investors remained cautious ahead of key Big Tech earnings, with focus on whether AI-driven momentum can sustain. Semiconductor pressure and earnings uncertainty kept overall sentiment fragile at the open.

items.title: External sectors steady despite geopolitical uncertainties: RBI
items.summary: India's external sector shows steady improvement, boosted by foreign investment inflows. The economy remains strong despite global uncertainties and trade disruptions. Foreign exchange reserves provide ample cover for imports and external debt. Exports and imports saw strong growth in the first quarter of this fiscal year. Bilateral trade agreements will further enhance India's external trade momentum.

items.title: Tariffs alone will not produce sustainable generic medicine in US: IPA
items.summary: The Indian Pharmaceutical Alliance believes tariffs alone will not build US generic drug manufacturing. Broader reforms are needed for fair pricing and supply chain stability. India supplies nearly fifty percent of generic medicines to the United States. Tariffs alone will not produce sustainable generic medicine onshoring. Reforms should address upstream buying groups and delayed generic competition.

items.title: India aims to ready five small nuclear reactors by 2033
items.summary: India plans to build at least five small modular reactors by 2033. These reactors will broaden nuclear power use and reduce coal reliance. Different designs are being developed for power generation and hydrogen production. The government has committed over $2 billion to develop these reactors. India aims for an eleven-fold expansion in nuclear installations by 2047.

items.title: Quote of the day by John Maynard Keynes: "Speculators may do no harm as bubbles on a steady stream of enterprise. But the position is serious when enterprise becomes the bubble on a whirlpool of speculation"
items.summary: John Maynard Keynes' quote highlights speculation's dual nature in markets. Healthy speculation supports enterprise, while excessive speculation can destabilize economies. Modern markets face risks as AI and cryptocurrencies can overshadow fundamentals.

The brand faced significant financial strain and regulatory setbacks over recent years. Production had halted, and employees raised concerns about unpaid salaries. The settlement aims to revive the once-popular craft beer market leader.

Enterprise clients now contribute a larger share of rental revenue, reflecting market demand. Global Capability Centres and multi-city clients are also showing increased contributions. Smartworks remains confident in delivering its guidance and creating long-term value.

Rising oil prices and supply fears intensified concerns about India's economic outlook. The Indian rupee weakened to a two-month low against the dollar. Overseas investors continue to purchase bonds, supporting the market.

items.title: NHAI fixes salary for engineers engaged with consultancy firms involved in national highways development
items.summary: National Highways Authority of India has mandated minimum pay for key professionals. Consultancy firms must retain twenty percent of sub-professional staff from government bodies. This move aims to enhance project delivery and improve quality standards. Revised provisions will strengthen technical capabilities of engaged consultancy firms. The framework seeks to leverage professional experience and provide employment opportunities.

items.summary: NH Studioz and Vinod Chopra Films have formed a significant long-term partnership. This collaboration will manage the distribution and syndication of Vidhu Vinod Chopra's extensive film library. Selected films will first return to theaters before wider platform releases. NH Studioz will represent over 5,000 films, reinforcing its industry leadership. This deal aims to bring classic Indian cinema to new audiences worldwide.

The combined order is valued at over Rs 400 crore for the national grid. This project strengthens Egypt's high voltage transmission backbone and supports economic demand. The company's MENA presence is growing with international undertaking.

items.title: Nestlé cooks up 35.6% export growth as Maggi finds new global shelves amid war jitters
items.summary: In the June quarter, Nestlé India achieved a remarkable export increase of 35.6%. The launch of new Maggi variants in Canada and Europe played a crucial role in amplifying its global reach. Furthermore, Nescafé Sunrise's entry into Lebanon marked a significant milestone. With total sales skyrocketing by 25.4%, driven by growing consumer loyalty and volume growth, the company proudly attained 4-Star Export House recognition for its substantial export efforts.

items.summary: In a strategic move to boost shareholder value after an impressive year, TVS Motor Company is exploring the separation of its financial services division. Additionally, TVS Motor is focused on advancing technology and product development for sustained growth.
